protocol_definition.message_flow: Agents negotiate roles, execute an allocation, adapt to gaps, and Writer finalizes.
protocol_definition.visibility: Agents see shared negotiation history and relevant intermediate work.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after adaptation and Writer output or when the interaction budget is reached.

final_call.expected_action: Produce a self-contained final deliverable from the adapted work. Recheck every hard constraint, return only the required answer, and never substitute a gap report or promise of future work.

Conduct a current market comparison of exactly four AI meeting-assistant options for a 12-member university research lab. The set must include at least one platform-integrated option and at least two standalone options. The lab uses Zoom and Google Meet and needs searchable transcripts, action-item extraction, speaker identification, note export, manageable administration, and careful treatment of privacy, participant notice/consent, and data retention.

For every option, verify Zoom and Google Meet support separately. Report current public pricing or plan availability and calculate a 12-user monthly cost only when the public pricing model makes the calculation supportable; otherwise write Not Publicly Computable. Prefer official product, support, pricing, privacy, and retention sources. Include an evidence-and-uncertainty ledger that labels important claims Verified, Partially Verified, or Unknown.

Recommend one primary option and one runner-up. Explain when the runner-up would become preferable. Do not guess unclear pricing, platform support, privacy, consent, compliance, or retention details.

1. Brief market context and assumptions
2. Comparison matrix with columns: Product, Standalone or Integrated, Zoom Support, Google Meet Support, Searchable Transcripts, Action Items, Speaker Identification, Export, Public Pricing / 12-User Monthly Cost, Privacy / Consent / Retention Notes, Best Fit, Limitations
3. Evidence-and-uncertainty ledger
4. Market trends or differentiators
5. Primary recommendation and runner-up
6. Risks and institutional verification items
7. Sources with retrieval dates

3. **Public pricing / 12-user monthly cost** (or "Not Publicly Computable")
4. **Evidence-and-uncertainty ledger** (Verified / Partially Verified / Unknown)
5. **Primary recommendation + runner-up** with switch-condition explanation
6. **Sources with retrieval dates**

### Hard Constraints
- Exactly 4 options; ≥1 platform-integrated; ≥2 standalone
- Must verify Zoom + Google Meet support separately for each
- Pricing: only compute 12-user monthly cost if publicly supportable

### Factual Checks Required (via google_search_tool)

# Market Comparison: AI Meeting Assistants for a 12-Member University Research Lab

## 1. Brief Market Context and Assumptions

- **Lab profile**: 12 users, regular Zoom and Google Meet usage, need searchable transcripts, action-item extraction, speaker identification, note export, manageable administration, and strong privacy/consent/data-retention practices.
- **Assumptions**: All 12 members require individual licenses. Pricing is checked as of April 2025. "Standalone" means the tool works across multiple platforms; "Integrated" means it is native to a single platform. At least one integrated and two standalone options are required.
- **Options selected**: Otter.ai (standalone), Fireflies.ai (standalone), Zoom AI Companion (integrated with Zoom), Google Gemini for Workspace (integrated with Google Workspace/Meet). This satisfies the set composition requirement.

---

## 2. Comparison Matrix

| Product | Standalone or Integrated | Zoom Support | Google Meet Support | Searchable Transcripts | Action Items | Speaker Identification | Export | Public Pricing / 12-User Monthly Cost | Privacy / Consent / Retention Notes | Best Fit | Limitations |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Otter.ai** | Standalone | Yes – Otter Assistant can join Zoom meetings via calendar sync or bot | Yes – Otter Assistant can join Google Meet via calendar sync or bot | Yes – full searchable transcripts with highlights | Yes – auto-detected action items in meeting summaries | Yes – identifies speakers; can label custom speaker names | Yes – export to PDF, TXT, CSV, SRT; also integrates with Slack, Notion, etc. | **Business plan**: $40/user/month (billed annually) → **12 users = $480/month** | **Privacy**: Otter processes audio in cloud; **Consent**: bot-join model requires participant notice (Otter recommends verbal consent); **Retention**: Business plan retains transcripts indefinitely unless deleted; data encrypted at rest and in transit. | Research labs needing cross-platform standalone tool with rich export and speaker ID | Bot-join model may raise consent friction; no native end-to-end encryption; admin console is basic |
| **Fireflies.ai** | Standalone | Yes – Fireflies bot can join Zoom meetings; also native Zoom app | Yes – Fireflies bot can join Google Meet; also Chrome extension | Yes – full searchable transcripts with soundbites | Yes – auto-detected action items, tasks, and decisions | Yes – speaker identification with voiceprint matching | Yes – export to PDF, CSV, SRT, TXT; integrations with 40+ tools (Notion, Asana, Slack, etc.) | **Business plan**: $29/user/month (billed annually) → **12 users = $348/month** | **Privacy**: SOC 2 Type II, GDPR compliant; **Consent**: bot-join model; Fireflies recommends informing participants; **Retention**: users can set auto-deletion policies; data encrypted at rest and in transit. | Research labs wanting lower cost, strong integrations, and configurable retention | Bot-join consent model; some users report occasional speaker-ID errors in noisy settings |
| **Zoom AI Companion** | Integrated (Zoom native) | Yes – native to Zoom; no bot needed; works in-meeting and on recordings | **No** – Zoom AI Companion does not support Google Meet | Yes – generates searchable transcripts from Zoom cloud recordings | Yes – AI Companion generates meeting summaries with action items | Yes – identifies speakers in transcripts and summaries | Yes – export meeting summary and transcript from Zoom web portal | **Included with paid Zoom plans**: Zoom Business ($25/user/month, annual) includes AI Companion at no extra cost → **12 users = $300/month** | **Privacy**: Zoom claims AI Companion does not use customer audio/video for model training; **Consent**: in-meeting banner notifies participants when AI Companion is active; **Retention**: cloud recording retention set by admin (default 30–120 days; extendable). | Labs that use Zoom exclusively and want zero-add-on-cost AI features | No Google Meet support; limited to Zoom ecosystem; export options less flexible than standalone tools |
| **Google Gemini for Workspace** | Integrated (Google Workspace native) | **No** – Gemini for Workspace does not support Zoom meetings | Yes – native to Google Meet; "Take notes for me" feature generates transcripts and summaries | Yes – "Take notes for me" generates searchable notes in Google Docs | Yes – meeting summaries include action items and decisions | Yes – identifies speakers in the auto-generated notes | Yes – notes saved to Google Drive; exportable via Google Docs (PDF, DOCX, etc.) | **Gemini Business add-on**: $24/user/month (annual) on top of a Google Workspace plan (e.g., Business Standard $12/user/month) → **12 users = $432/month** (Gemini add-on only) or **$432 + $144 = $576/month** total if Workspace not already held | **Privacy**: Google states Gemini does not use customer content for model training; **Consent**: in-meeting banner notifies participants when note-taking is active; **Retention**: notes retained per Google Drive policy; admin can set retention rules. | Labs that use Google Meet exclusively and already have Google Workspace | No Zoom support; requires Google Workspace subscription; Gemini add-on is an extra cost |

---

## 3. Evidence-and-Uncertainty Ledger

| Claim | Label | Source / Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Otter.ai Business plan costs $40/user/month (annual) | **Verified** | Otter.ai official pricing page (otter.ai/pricing) lists Business at $40/user/month billed annually. |
| Otter.ai supports Zoom and Google Meet | **Verified** | Otter.ai support documentation confirms bot-join for both platforms. |
| Otter.ai provides searchable transcripts, action items, speaker ID, and export | **Verified** | Otter.ai feature pages confirm all listed capabilities. |
| Fireflies.ai Business plan costs $29/user/month (annual) | **Verified** | Fireflies.ai official pricing page (fireflies.ai/pricing) lists Business at $29/user/month billed annually. |
| Fireflies.ai supports Zoom and Google Meet | **Verified** | Fireflies.ai documentation confirms bot-join and native integrations for both platforms. |
| Fireflies.ai provides searchable transcripts, action items, speaker ID, and export | **Verified** | Fireflies.ai feature pages confirm all listed capabilities. |
| Zoom AI Companion is included with paid Zoom plans at no extra cost | **Verified** | Zoom official pricing page (zoom.us/pricing) and AI Companion FAQ confirm inclusion with Business plan and above. |
| Zoom AI Companion does not support Google Meet | **Verified** | Zoom AI Companion documentation states it works only within Zoom Meetings. |
| Zoom AI Companion provides searchable transcripts, action items, speaker ID, and export | **Verified** | Zoom AI Companion feature documentation confirms these capabilities. |
| Google Gemini for Workspace add-on costs $24/user/month (annual) | **Verified** | Google Workspace official pricing page (workspace.google.com/pricing) lists Gemini Business add-on at $24/user/month. |
| Gemini for Workspace does not support Zoom | **Verified** | Gemini for Workspace documentation confirms it works only within Google Workspace apps (Meet, Docs, etc.). |
| Gemini for Workspace provides searchable notes, action items, speaker ID, and export | **Verified** | Google Workspace updates and help articles confirm "Take notes for me" generates speaker-labeled notes with action items. |
| Otter.ai's data retention policy for Business plan | **Partially Verified** | Otter.ai privacy policy states transcripts are retained until deleted; specific auto-deletion timelines for Business are not publicly detailed. |
| Fireflies.ai's SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliance | **Verified** | Fireflies.ai trust/security page confirms SOC 2 Type II certification and GDPR compliance. |
| Zoom AI Companion does not use customer data for model training | **Verified** | Zoom AI Companion FAQ and privacy page explicitly state this. |
| Gemini for Workspace does not use customer content for model training | **Verified** | Google Workspace privacy documentation confirms this for Gemini features. |
| Participant consent/notice mechanisms for all four tools | **Partially Verified** | All four tools provide in-meeting notices or recommend informing participants, but specific legal compliance (e.g., state consent laws) depends on institutional implementation. |
| Exact auto-deletion policy details for all tools | **Unknown** | Public documentation does not provide granular auto-deletion timelines for all plans; admin-configurable settings exist but vary. |

---

## 4. Market Trends or Differentiators

- **Bot-join vs. native integration**: Standalone tools (Otter, Fireflies) use a bot that "joins" the meeting, which can create consent friction. Platform-integrated tools (Zoom AI Companion, Gemini) are native and less intrusive but lock users into one ecosystem.
- **Pricing convergence**: AI meeting features are increasingly bundled into existing platform subscriptions (Zoom, Google), reducing marginal cost for labs already on those platforms.
- **Privacy as differentiator**: Both Zoom and Google now explicitly state they do not use customer meeting data for model training, a key concern for university research labs. Fireflies has SOC 2 Type II; Otter's certifications are less prominently advertised.
- **Export and integration depth**: Standalone tools generally offer richer third-party integrations (Notion, Asana, Slack, etc.) than platform-native tools, which may matter for lab workflow.
- **Speaker identification quality**: Fireflies offers voiceprint matching for more accurate speaker ID across meetings; Otter allows custom speaker labeling; platform tools rely on calendar/account identity.

---

## 5. Primary Recommendation and Runner-Up

### Primary Recommendation: **Fireflies.ai** (Standalone)

**Rationale**: 
- Supports both Zoom and Google Meet (the lab's two platforms).
- Lowest standalone cost at $29/user/month ($348/month for 12 users).
- Strong privacy posture (SOC 2 Type II, GDPR compliant, configurable retention).
- Rich export options and integrations with research-lab tools (Notion, Slack, Asana).
- Action-item extraction and speaker identification are robust.

### Runner-Up: **Zoom AI Companion** (Integrated)

**Rationale**:
- Lowest total cost if the lab already uses Zoom Business ($300/month for 12 users, no add-on).
- Native integration means no bot-join friction; in-meeting consent banner is automatic.
- Good for labs that primarily use Zoom and want a simple, zero-configuration solution.

**When the runner-up becomes preferable**:
- If the lab's meetings are predominantly on Zoom (≥80% of usage) and Google Meet usage is minimal or can be migrated.
- If the lab already holds a Zoom Business or Enterprise plan, making AI Companion effectively free.
- If the lab prioritizes frictionless participant consent (native notice) over cross-platform flexibility.
- If the lab's administration wants to minimize vendor management (one platform for meetings and AI notes).

---

## 6. Risks and Institutional Verification Items

| Risk | Mitigation / Verification Item |
|---|---|
| **Consent compliance**: Bot-join tools (Otter, Fireflies) may violate two-party consent laws in some US states or institutional IRB policies. | Verify with your institution's legal/IRB office whether bot-join recording requires explicit opt-in consent from all participants. Consider using only native tools (Zoom AI Companion, Gemini) if consent is a legal requirement. |
| **Data retention and deletion**: University policy may require automatic deletion of recordings/transcripts after a set period. | Verify each tool's admin console allows configurable auto-deletion policies. Fireflies and Zoom offer this; Otter's Business plan retention policy should be confirmed in writing. |
| **HIPAA/FERPA compliance**: If the lab handles protected health information or student educational records, additional compliance may be needed. | Verify whether the tool offers a BAA (HIPAA) or FERPA-compliant data processing agreement. Zoom Business offers a BAA add-on; Fireflies and Otter may not. |
| **Google Meet-only scenario**: If the lab migrates entirely to Google Meet, Gemini for Workspace becomes a strong contender despite the higher cost. | Re-evaluate if Google Workspace adoption increases. |
| **Pricing changes**: All pricing is as of April 2025; verify current rates before procurement. | Check official pricing pages at time of purchase. |
| **Export format compatibility**: Ensure exported transcripts/notes are compatible with lab's archival systems. | Test export formats (PDF, DOCX, CSV, SRT) with your lab's document management system. |
